feat: GitHub device flow auth — no callback URL needed

Replaces redirect-based OAuth with device flow for portable auth
that works on any port/machine/container.

Backend:
- AuthService: startDeviceFlow() + pollDeviceFlow() methods
- POST /api/auth/device — returns user_code + verification_uri
- POST /api/auth/device/poll — polls GitHub, sets JWT cookie on approval

Frontend:
- Auth store: startDeviceFlow(), cancelDeviceFlow(), deviceFlow state
- LoginPage: shows code + github.com/login/device link, polls until approved
- No redirect, no callback URL registration needed

Flow: Click Login → see code ABCD-1234 → go to github.com/login/device →
enter code → approve → app auto-detects and logs in.

commit.message:
feat: add web UI dashboard for live traffic inspection (#2)

* Initial plan

* feat: add live web UI dashboard for request/response inspection

Starts an HTTP server on http://localhost:7676 (configurable with
--ui-port) whenever `porter http` is run.

The dashboard shows live tunnelled traffic with:
- Method-coloured request list (newest first)
- Per-request detail panel: request headers, request body,
  response status, response headers, response body
- Pretty-printed JSON bodies
- Response timing
- SSE-based live updates + event-history replay for late joiners
- Clear button

Co-authored-by: SandeepOCT <139792713+SandeepOCT@users.noreply.github.com>

* Centralize tunnel frame handling; export Frame type

Import and use the shared Frame type and introduce a tunnelFrame helper to centralize encoding/sending frames and emitting UI events. Agent now only writes response frames back over the socket (prevents request spoofing) and emits mapped events via getEventName/getEventPayload. Refactored proxy callbacks and request handling to use tunnelFrame instead of direct socket.write calls, and changed response start/data/end payload shapes accordingly. Also export the Frame type from buffer.ts to make frame typing available across modules.

* Update agent.ts

* refactor(ui): rewrite dashboard with HTMX + Hyperscript

Replace the 190-line vanilla JS block with server-driven HTML.

Server changes (src/ui/server.ts):
- Add RequestRecord state map; server is now rendering source-of-truth
- SSE pushes HTMX OOB HTML fragments instead of JSON
- New routes: GET /request/:id (detail panel), DELETE /requests (clear)
- New routes: GET /vendor/{htmx,sse,hyperscript}.js (serve libs locally)
- Vendor JS (htmx.org, htmx-ext-sse, hyperscript.org) loaded from
  node_modules via require.resolve; NCC bundles them as dist/ assets

HTML changes:
- Load HTMX + htmx-ext-sse + Hyperscript from /vendor/* (no CDN)
- hx-ext="sse" sse-connect="/events" on <body>
- Hidden #sse-sink absorbs ui-update events and applies OOB patches
- Each row: hx-get="/request/{id}" hx-target="#detail-panel" hx-trigger="click"
- .detail-content wrapper carries sse-swap="response-end-{id}" so the
  detail panel auto-refreshes when the response completes (no JS needed)
- Clear button: hx-delete="/requests"; server response carries OOB patches
  to reset list panel, detail panel, and count in one round-trip
- Hyperscript _= on <body>: handles connection-status dot/label
- Hyperscript _= on rows: manages .active class without any JS

Other fixes:
- Remove redundant timestamp overrides in agent.ts getEventPayload
- Add --ui-port validation in command/index.ts
- Add htmx.org, htmx-ext-sse, hyperscript.org as dependencies

Co-authored-by: SandeepOCT <139792713+SandeepOCT@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(ui): load HTMX and Hyperscript via CDN script links

Replace local vendor file serving (readFileSync + /vendor/* routes)
with standard CDN <script> tags pointing to unpkg.com.

Each tag includes integrity (SRI sha384) and crossorigin="anonymous"
attributes so browsers verify script integrity before executing.

- Remove readFileSync import and entire vendor-loading block
  (require.resolve paths, safeReadVendor helper, HTMX_JS/SSE_JS/
   HYPERSCRIPT_JS constants)
- Replace <script src="/vendor/..."> with unpkg.com CDN links
- Remove GET /vendor/htmx.js, /vendor/sse.js, /vendor/hyperscript.js
  HTTP route handlers from startUIServer
- Uninstall htmx.org, htmx-ext-sse, hyperscript.org npm dependencies
- Remove src/ui/vendor/ entry from .gitignore

Bundle size: 323 kB → 164 kB (no vendor assets bundled)

Co-authored-by: SandeepOCT <139792713+SandeepOCT@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ui): make request rows always clickable during live SSE traffic

Root cause: per-row hx-get/hx-trigger/hx-swap/_= attributes require
htmx.process() to be called after each SSE OOB swap to register the
click handler.  The htmx-ext-sse extension does not guarantee this
call when inserting/replacing rows via hx-swap-oob, leaving rows
without click listeners during live traffic.

Fix: replace per-element HTMX/Hyperscript attributes with a single
event-delegation click listener on the stable #list-panel container.
Since #list-panel is never itself replaced by SSE swaps, one listener
covers all current and future row elements regardless of OOB activity.
Uses htmx.ajax() for the detail-panel request so the response HTML is
still processed by HTMX (enabling sse-swap on .detail-content).

Co-authored-by: SandeepOCT <139792713+SandeepOCT@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor UI server; add Channel, Storage, HTML modules

Modularize and refactor the in-browser UI/server plumbing:

- Introduce Channel (SSE) abstraction to manage SSE clients and message formatting.
- Add Storage<T> for storing server-side request records (size/lookup/iteration API).
- Move HTML rendering into src/ui/html (content + utils) and export INDEX_HTML, row/detail fragments and helpers.
- Add RequestRecord class/type in src/ui/types to encapsulate request lifecycle and body/header accumulation.
- Update src/ui/server.ts to use Channel, Storage and the new HTML modules; centralize agent event handling via processEvent and EventType iteration, and replay current state to new SSE connections.
- Minor updates to util files (agent/buffer) to integrate with new event flow.

These changes separate concerns (storage, SSE, rendering), simplify event processing, and make server-side UI code more maintainable and testable.

---------

Co-authored-by: SandeepOCT <139792713+SandeepOCT@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

feat(rust): add Kuzu graph database support via PyO3 bridge

Add KuzuGraphStore implementing the GraphStore trait, backed by Kuzu's
Python API through PyO3. Also add KuzuBackend implementing MemoryBackend
and ExperienceBackend traits.

Key design decisions:
- Optional feature flag: 'kuzu' (depends on pyo3)
- Dynamic schema with ensure_node_table/ensure_rel_table
- Parameterized Cypher queries via PyDict
- Thread safety via Mutex
- max_db_size capped at 1GB to avoid 8TB mmap issues

New modules (behind #[cfg(feature = "kuzu")]):
- graph/kuzu_store.rs: Full GraphStore implementation with 13 tests
- backends/kuzu_backend.rs: MemoryBackend + ExperienceBackend with 6 tests

All 242 existing tests pass without the kuzu feature.
All 261 tests pass with the kuzu feature enabled.

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

against KNOWN_NAMES. Multi-word entries like "san suu kyi", "de jong",

tokens are not in the list. Removed all 50 multi-word entries.
